sections.label: Section 1273
sections.original: καλῶς ἂν εἴη Μενέλεῴ τε πρὸς χάριν .
sections.translation: That would be well done , and pleasing to Menelaos , too .

sections.label: Section 1274
sections.original: οὔκουν σὺ χωρὶς τῆσδε δρῶν ἀρκεῖς τάδε ;
sections.translation: Can you not perform these rites well enough without Helen ?

sections.label: Section 1275
sections.original: μητρὸς τόδʼ ἔργον ἢ γυναικὸς ἢ τέκνων .
sections.translation: This task belongs to mother , or wife , or children .

sections.label: Section 1276
sections.original: ταύτης ὁ μόχθος , ὡς λέγεις , θάπτειν πόσιν .
sections.translation: According to you , the work of burying her husband belongs to her .

sections.label: Section 1277
sections.original: ἐν εὐσεβεῖ γοῦν νόμιμα μὴ κλέπτειν νεκρῶν .
sections.translation: Yes indeed ; piety demands that the dead be not robbed of their due .

sections.label: Section 1278
sections.original: ἴτω · πρὸς ἡμῶν ἄλοχον εὐσεβῆ τρέφειν .
sections.translation: Let her go ; it is in my interest to foster piety in a wife . Go inside and choose adornment for the dead ;

sections.label: Section 1280
sections.original: καὶ σὲ οὐ κεναῖσι χερσὶ γῆς ἀποστελῶ ,
sections.translation: I will not send you away from the land empty-handed either , since you have done her a favor . As you have brought me good news , you will receive clothing instead of going in rags , and food , so that you may reach your country , since now I see you doing very badly indeed .

sections.label: Section 1285
sections.original: σὺ δʼ , ὦ τάλαινα , μὴ ʼπὶ τοῖς ἀνηνύτοις
sections.translation: As for you , poor lady , do not wear yourself out in a hopeless case ; Menelaos has met his doom , and your dead husband could not return to life .

sections.label: Section 1288
sections.original: σὸν ἔργον , ὦ νεᾶνι · τὸν παρόντα μὲν
sections.translation: This is your duty , young woman ; you must be content with the husband at your side , and let go the one that no longer exists ;

sections.label: Section 1290
sections.original: ἄριστα γάρ σοι ταῦτα πρὸς τὸ τυγχάνον .
sections.translation: for this is best for you , according to what has happened . And if I come to Hellas and find safety , I will put to an end your former bad reputation , if you are such a wife as you ought to be to your husband .

sections.label: Section 1294
sections.original: ἔσται τάδʼ · οὐδὲ μέμψεται πόσις ποτὲ
sections.translation: I will ; my husband will never find fault with me ;

sections.label: Section 1295
sections.original: ἡμῖν · σὺ δʼ αὐτὸς ἐγγὺς ὢν εἴσῃ τάδε .
sections.translation: you yourself will be at hand to know it . Now go inside , unhappy man , and find the bath , and change your clothes . I will show my kindness to you without delay . For you will perform the due services with more kindly feeling for my dearest Menelaos ,

sections.label: Section 1300
sections.original: δρῴης ἄν , ἡμῶν τυγχάνων οἵων σε χρή .
sections.translation: if you get from me what you ought to have . Exeunt Theoklymenos , Helen , Menelaos .

sections.label: Section 1.1301
sections.original: Ὀρεία ποτὲ δρομάδι κώ-
sections.translation: Once with swift foot the mountain mother of the gods rushed through the wooded glen , and the river ’ s streams

sections.label: Section 1.1305
sections.original: βαρύβρομόν τε κῦμʼ ἅλιον
sections.translation: and the deep-thundering sea wave , yearning for her lost daughter , whose name may not be spoken . The loudly rattling castanets cried out a shrill sound ,

sections.label: Section 1.1310
sections.original: θηρῶν ὅτε ζυγίους
sections.translation: when they , swift-footed as whirlwinds , followed the goddess on her chariot yoked to wild creatures , after the girl that was snatched away from the circling chorus of maidens —

sections.label: Section 1.1315
sections.original: ἃ μὲν τόξοις Ἄρτεμις , ἃ δʼ
sections.translation: here Artemis with her bow , and there the grim-eyed goddess , in full armor , with her spear . But Zeus , who sees clearly from his throne in heaven , brought to pass another destiny .

sections.label: Section 1.1319
sections.original: δρομαῖον δʼ ὅτε πολυπλάνη-
sections.translation: When the mother ceased from her wild

sections.label: Section 1.1320
sections.original: τον μάτηρ ἔπαυσε πόνον ,
sections.translation: wandering toil , searching for the treacherous rape of her daughter , she crossed the snow-capped heights of the nymphs of Mount Ida ;

sections.label: Section 1.1325
sections.original: ῥίπτει δʼ ἐν πένθει
sections.translation: and in sorrow cast herself down in the rocky woods deep in snow ; and , by not making fruitful with crops the barren fields of the earth for mortals , she destroyed the human race .

sections.label: Section 1.1330
sections.original: ποίμναις δʼ οὐχ ἵει θαλερὰς
sections.translation: She would not send forth the rich nourishment of leafy tendrils for the herds , and life was leaving the cities . No sacrifice was offered to the gods , and on the altars were no cakes to burn ;

sections.label: Section 1.1335
sections.original: πηγὰς δʼ ἀμπαύει δροσερὰς
sections.translation: she made the dew-fed springs of clear water cease flowing , the avenger in sorrow for her child .

sections.label: Section 2.1338
sections.original: ἐπεὶ δʼ ἔπαυσʼ εἰλαπίνας
sections.translation: When she made an end to banquets for gods and the race of men , Zeus spoke out , appeasing the

sections.label: Section 2.1340
sections.original: Ζεὺς μειλίσσων στυγίους
sections.translation: Mother ’ s gloomy wrath : Go , holy Graces , go and with a loud cry take from Demeter ’ s angry heart her grief for the maiden ;

sections.label: Section 2.1345
sections.original: Μοῦσαί θʼ ὕμνοισι χορῶν .
sections.translation: and you , Muses , with song and dance . Then Kypris , fairest of the blessed gods , first took up the rumbling voice of bronze and the drum with tight-stretched skin ; and the goddess smiled ,

sections.label: Section 2.1350
sections.original: δέξατό τʼ ἐς χέρας
sections.translation: and received in her hand the deep-toned flute , pleased with its loud note .

sections.label: Section 2.1353
sections.original: ὧν οὐ θέμις σʼ οὔθʼ ὁσία
sections.translation: You made burnt offerings that were neither right nor holy , in the chambers of the gods ,

sections.label: Section 2.1355
sections.original: μῆνιν δʼ ἔσχες μεγάλας
sections.translation: and you have incurred the wrath of the great mother , child , by not honoring her sacrifices . Oh ! Great is the power of dappled fawn-skin robes ,

sections.label: Section 2.1360
sections.original: κισσοῦ τε στεφθεῖσα χλόα
sections.translation: and green ivy that crowns a sacred thyrsos , the whirling beat of the tambourine circling in the air , hair streaming wildly for the revelry of Bromios ,

sections.label: Section 2.1365
sections.original: ῳ καὶ παννυχίδες θεᾶς .
sections.translation: and the night-long festivals of the goddess . . . . You gloried in your beauty alone .

sections.label: Section 1369
sections.original: τὰ μὲν κατʼ οἴκους εὐτυχοῦμεν , ὦ φίλαι ·
sections.translation: My friends , all goes well for us inside ;

sections.label: Section 1370
sections.original: ἡ γὰρ συνεκκλέπτουσα Πρωτέως κόρη
sections.translation: for the daughter of Proteus , who aids us in our theft , has told her brother nothing when questioned as to my husband ’ s coming ; for my sake she said that he was not alive , but dead and buried . . . . My husband has snatched up by chance fine things indeed ;

sections.label: Section 1375
sections.original: ἃ γὰρ καθήσειν ὅπλʼ ἔμελλεν εἰς ἅλα ,
sections.translation: for he is carrying away the armor he was intending to let fall into the sea , putting his noble arm through the shield-strap and holding a spear in his right hand , on pretence of joining in the service to the dead . He has equipped his body with weapons conveniently for the battle ,

sections.label: Section 1380
sections.original: ὡς βαρβάρων τρόπαια μυρίων χερὶ
sections.translation: in order to to set up the trophies of countless barbarians , whenever we embark on the oared ship . I adorned him with robes in place of his shipwrecked garments , and I washed him , a long-delayed bath in water from the stream .

sections.label: Section 1385
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , ἐκπερᾷ γὰρ δωμάτων ὁ τοὺς ἐμοὺς
sections.translation: But I must be silent , for the man is coming from the house who thinks he holds my marriage ready in his hands ; and I claim your goodwill and strict silence , so that , when we have saved ourselves , we may be able some day to save you also .

sections.label: Section 1390
sections.original: χωρεῖτʼ ἐφεξῆς , ὡς ἔταξεν ὁ ξένος ,
sections.translation: Advance in order , servants , as the stranger directed , bearing the funeral gifts for the sea . But you , Helen , if you will agree with my words , be persuaded and stay here ; for you will do your husband equal service whether you are present or not .

sections.label: Section 1395
sections.original: δέδοικα γάρ σε μή τις ἐμπεσὼν πόθος
sections.translation: For I am afraid that some sudden passion should persuade you to throw your body into the swelling waves , stricken by love for your former husband ; you are grieving for him too much , although he is lost .

sections.label: Section 1399
sections.original: ὦ καινὸς ἡμῖν πόσις , ἀναγκαίως ἔχει
sections.translation: O my new husband , I must honor

sections.label: Section 1400
sections.original: τὰ πρῶτα λέκτρα νυμφικάς θʼ ὁμιλίας
sections.translation: my first marriage-bed and the one whose company I shared as a bride ; for I could even die with my husband , I loved him so much . But how could he thank me , if I were to share death with him ? Let me go and pay funeral rites to the dead in person .

sections.label: Section 1405
sections.original: θεοὶ δὲ σοί τε δοῖεν οἷʼ ἐγὼ θέλω ,
sections.translation: May the gods give to you the things I wish and also to this stranger here , for his assistance ! And you will have in me such a wife at home as you ought to have , since you are doing a good service to Menelaos and to me ; for surely these events are leading to some good fortune .

sections.label: Section 1410
sections.original: ὅστις δὲ δώσει ναῦν ἐν ᾗ τάδʼ ἄξομεν ,
sections.translation: But now appoint someone to give us a ship in which to carry these gifts , so that I may have your kindness in full .

sections.label: Section 1412
sections.original: χώρει σὺ καὶ ναῦν τοῖσδε πεντηκόντορον
sections.translation: ( to an attendant .) You , go and give them a Sidonian ship of fifty oars , and rowers also .

sections.label: Section 1414
sections.original: οὔκουν ὅδʼ ἄρξει ναὸς ὃς κοσμεῖ τάφον ;
sections.translation: This man who is ordering the funeral will be in command of the ship , won ’ t he ?

sections.label: Section 1415
sections.original: μάλιστʼ · ἀκούειν τοῦδε χρὴ ναύτας ἐμούς .
sections.translation: Most certainly ; my sailors must obey him .

sections.label: Section 1416
sections.original: αὖθις κέλευσον , ἵνα σαφῶς μάθωσί σου .
sections.translation: Repeat the order , so that they may clearly understand you .

sections.label: Section 1417
sections.original: αὖθις κελεύω καὶ τρίτον γʼ , εἴ σοι φίλον .
sections.translation: I repeat the order , and a third time too , if you wish it .

sections.label: Section 1418
sections.original: ὄναιο · κἀγὼ τῶν ἐμῶν βουλευμάτων .
sections.translation: May you have benefit from it — and I from my plans !

sections.label: Section 1419
sections.original: μή νυν ἄγαν σὸν δάκρυσιν ἐκτήξῃς χρόα .
sections.translation: Do not ruin your skin with too much weeping now .

sections.label: Section 1420
sections.original: ἥδʼ ἡμέρα σοι τὴν ἐμὴν δείξει χάριν .
sections.translation: This day will show my gratitude to you .

sections.label: Section 1421
sections.original: τὰ τῶν θανόντων οὐδέν , ἀλλʼ ἄλλως πόνος .
sections.translation: The state of the dead is nothingness ; toil for them is vain .

sections.label: Section 1422
sections.original: ἔστιν τι κἀκεῖ κἀνθάδʼ ὧν ἐγὼ λέγω .
sections.translation: There is something of what I say both there and here .

sections.label: Section 1423
sections.original: οὐδὲν κακίω Μενέλεώ μʼ ἕξεις πόσιν .
sections.translation: You will not find in me a husband at all inferior to Menelaos .

sections.label: Section 1424
sections.original: οὐδὲν σὺ μεμπτός · τῆς τύχης με δεῖ μόνον .
sections.translation: You are not at fault in any way ; good luck is all I need .

sections.label: Section 1425
sections.original: ἐν σοὶ τόδʼ , ἢν σὴν εἰς ἔμʼ εὔνοιαν διδῷς .
sections.translation: That is in your power , if you show kindness to me .

sections.label: Section 1426
sections.original: οὐ νῦν διδαξόμεσθα τοὺς φίλους φιλεῖν .
sections.translation: This is not a lesson I shall have to learn now , to love my friends .

sections.label: Section 1427
sections.original: βούλῃ ξυνεργῶν αὐτὸς ἐκπέμψω στόλον ;
sections.translation: Would you like me to send out the equipment in person , working together with you ?

sections.label: Section 1428
sections.original: ἥκιστα · μὴ δούλευε σοῖς δούλοις , ἄναξ .
sections.translation: Not at all ! Do not become a slave to your slaves , lord .

sections.label: Section 1429
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἶα · τοὺς μὲν Πελοπιδῶν ἐῶ νόμους ·
sections.translation: Come then ! I have no concern with the customs of the race of Pelops .

sections.label: Section 1430
sections.original: καθαρὰ γὰρ ἡμῖν δώματʼ · οὐ γὰρ ἐνθάδε
sections.translation: My house is pure ; for Menelaos did not die here . Let someone go and tell my chieftans to bring marriage-offerings to my house ; the whole earth must ring with joyful wedding-songs

sections.label: Section 1435
sections.original: ὑμέναιον Ἑλένης κἀμόν , ὡς ζηλωτὸς ᾖ .
sections.translation: in celebration of my wedding with Helen , so that it may be envied . You , stranger , go and give to the sea ’ s embrace these offerings to this woman ’ s husband , who was once alive ; and then hurry back home with my wife , so that after sharing with me her marriage-feast , you may
